data.title: Key facts: Infosys beats Q4 profit; FY27 1.5–3.5% guidance; CEO equity
data.description:
• Infosys forecasts FY27 revenue growth of 1.5%–3.5% and operating margin of 20%–22%; cites AI and Europe as main revenue drivers, with AI services >5.5% of revenue.1
  • Infosys Q4: net profit ₹85.01B vs est ₹74.65B; revenue ₹464.02B, +13.4% YoY. Demand from banking, energy and communications drove the revenue gain.23
  • Choice reiterated BUY on Infosys (INFY), cut target to INR 1,500. Cited stable quarterly results, record $15bn deal wins, 24% TCV growth, modest near‑term revenue growth ~3.5% and FY27 ~2.5% CC.4
  • INFY announced partnerships to modernize supply chains, upgrade digital infrastructure and banking IT, and scale autonomous and agentic AI deployments with multiple tech and research partners.5
  • Infosys said sequential deal wins fell because last quarter included a single $1+ billion contract; absence of a comparable $1B+ deal this quarter cut reported deal value.6
  • Infosys Q4: US dollar revenue and EBIT slightly below CNBC-TV18 poll; rupee revenue matched poll. Reported earnings marginally softer than survey for the quarter.7
  • Infosys granted equity awards to CEO Salil Parekh effective May 2, 2026, across performance, ESG-linked, TSR and ownership programs; vesting subject to conditions and units tied to market price.8

data.title: Scandinavia's Kitron beats Q1 estimates, says trending to upper half of FY forecast
data.description:
Overview

  • Scandinavia electronics manufacturer reported Q1 revenue up 66% yr/yr, beating analyst expectations

  • Q1 EBIT rose and beat analyst estimates, with margin improving to 9.4%

  • Order backlog hit record EUR 805.9 mln, up 54% yr/yr on strong Defence/Aerospace demand

Outlook

  • Kitron maintains 2026 revenue outlook of EUR 900-1050 mln, EBIT of EUR 84-108 mln

  • Company says figures are trending towards upper half of 2026 guidance ranges

  • Kitron sees strong demand and a deep pipeline for 2026 and 2027

Result Drivers

  • DEFENCE/AEROSPACE DEMAND - Co said accelerating demand in Defence/Aerospace sector was primary driver of record Q1 revenue and profit

  • BROAD-BASED SECTOR GROWTH - All market sectors grew compared to a year ago, according to co

  • STRONG CUSTOMER DEMAND - CEO said demand has rarely been stronger across customer base and pipeline of new opportunities is deepest ever

Company press release:

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy", 4 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the electronic equipment & parts peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Kitron ASA is NOK110.00, about 14.2% above its April 23 closing price of NOK96.30

  • The stock recently traded at 24 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 21 three months ago

data.title: Car parts group Forvia's quarterly revenue drops on lower China sales
data.description:
French car parts supplier Forvia on Friday reported a 2.2% drop in its first-quarter sales, excluding currency effects, impacted by a decline in China.

Quarterly sales fell to 5.14 billion euros ($6.00 billion) from 5.49 billion euros in the same period last year.

The group reported growth in all of its regions except China, where sales were down 23.5% from last year, excluding the effects of currency exchange rates.

Forvia was impacted by an unfavourable customer mix in China, with a significant drop in production at automaker BYD, it said.

It still outperformed the 3.4% decline seen in global automotive production, according to S&P Global Mobility forecasts published this month.

"At the same time, we have continued to make progress on the planned divestiture of our Interiors business, which we expect to materialize in the near term," CEO Martin Fischer said in a press release.

The company also confirmed its guidance for 2026.

($1 = 0.8563 euros)

data.title: Singapore's Consumer Inflation Could Still Accelerate — Market Talk
data.description: Singapore's consumer inflation could continue to accelerate, even though it picked up speed in March, OCBC's Selena Ling says in a note. March's headline and core inflation rose to 1.8% on year and 1.7% on month, up from February's rise of 1.2% and 1.4%, respectively. There could be inflationary pass-through from 2Q, as the prolonged war in the Middle East and continued closure of the Strait of Hormuz trigger higher energy and petrochemical-related costs for businesses. OCBC expects 2Q headline and core inflation to accelerate further to 2%, with private transport costs, utilities, air travel and food costs feeling the spillover effects from the war and supply chain disruptions. (amanda.lee@wsj.com)

data.title: Medical-Device Firm Star Sports Medicine Seeks $106 Mln in Hong Kong IPO
data.description:
By Jason Chau

Chinese medical-device maker Star Sports Medicine has started taking orders for its initial public offering in Hong Kong, seeking to tap strong investor appetite for China's emerging industries.

Star Sports Medicine seeks to raise up to 829.6 million Hong Kong dollars, equivalent to US$105.9 million, in an offering of 8.4 million shares priced at HK$98.50 each.

The Beijing-based company plans to use the net proceeds to expand production capacity, fund research and development, and strengthen its sales and marketing efforts to reinforce its position in China and enhance its global branding, according to its exchange filing Friday.

The offering is set to close on April 29, with shares expected to start trading on May 5.

Star Sports Medicine has a portfolio of 63 products spanning medical implants, consumables and surgical instruments used in the treatment and rehabilitation of sports-related injuries.

China's sports medicine device industry remains at an early stage of development, which Star Sports says offers significant growth potential compared with more developed countries. The Chinese market is projected to expand at a 16.5% compound annual growth rate from 2024 to 2030, the company said in the filing, citing data from consulting firm China Insights Industry Consultancy.

The medical-device maker recorded a steady increase in revenue and gross profit for the past three years, its filing showed.

The offering adds to a banner year for IPOs in Hong Kong. The city's exchange said recently that it has nearly 500 listings in the pipeline.

Write to Jason Chau at jason.chau@wsj.com
